C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.message: Avoid hardcoding passwords in source code; use environment variables or secret stores.
C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.explanation: This file contains a hardcoded secret or credential in source code which can be read by anyone with repository access.

C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.message: Use of eval() can lead to code injection or unexpected behavior. Avoid using it with untrusted input.
C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.explanation: Use of functions like eval() or exec() can execute arbitrary code and should be avoided, especially on user-controlled inputs.
C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.fix: Replace eval/exec with safer alternatives. For parsing expressions use ast.literal_eval or write a simple parser. Validate inputs strictly.

C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.message: Use of subprocess APIs can run external commands; ensure inputs are sanitized. Detected shell=True which increases risk of injection.
C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.explanation: Calling subprocess APIs with unsanitized inputs or with shell=True can allow command injection or execution of unintended commands.
C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.fix: Avoid shell=True and pass arguments as a list. Validate and sanitize any inputs used in command construction.

C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.message: Use of md5 is deprecated for security-sensitive hashing. Use sha256 or stronger algorithms.
C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.explanation: MD5 and SHA1 are considered cryptographically broken or weak for collision resistance and should not be used for security-sensitive hashing.
C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.fix: Use hashlib.sha256 or a stronger function and use salt + PBKDF2 / bcrypt / scrypt / Argon2 for password hashing.

C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.message: Detected SQL execution using a variable that appears to be built via string formatting/concatenation. Use parameterized queries.
C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.explanation: This code constructs SQL statements by concatenating strings or by formatting them directly. Attackers can inject SQL fragments through inputs, leading to data leakage or corruption.
C:\\Users\\HP\\Desktop\\CodeGuardian\\in.fix: Use parameterized queries (e.g., cursor.execute(sql, params)) or ORM query builders to avoid direct string composition of SQL. Validate and sanitize inputs.
